notes: Day 22 (Mar 21 = NOWRUZ DAY 2) UPDATE 4:15AM ET. Sources: CNN D20-D22, AJ liveblog D19-D22, CNBC, AP, Bloomberg, Fortune, OilPrice.com, OilPriceAPI, NPR, NBC, Wikipedia, Investing.com, IAEA, Euronews, NCRI, Iran International, Carnegie, Windward, PBS, UNHCR, Zelenskyy, Hengaw 5th report, FDD, HRW, JPost, Fox News, Britannica, NetBlocks, Militarnyi, TWZ, USNI News, EU Kallas, HRANA, Red Cross, ACLED, IranWire, Queen Farah Pahlavi, The National, Stimson Center, Gulf International Forum, Amnesty International, Washington Post, Axios, Richard Haass/Substack.

NOWRUZ DAY 2 — MOJTABA STILL ABSENT: Second day of Nowruz with no video appearance. Succession crisis deepening. Telegraph leaked audio: Mojtaba survived with 'minor leg injury' — but continued absence contradicts. Araghchi claims 'excellent health' while regime produces zero proof. Iranian media attempting to cast Khamenei Jr as 'wartime leader' — but invisible wartime leaders lose authority daily. IRGC attempting to fill vacuum with written statements.

HOUTHI RESTRAINT — MAJOR STRATEGIC FINDING: Despite expectations, Houthis have NOT entered the war after 22 days. The National: 'mystery of no Houthi attacks.' AP (Mar 15): Houthi stockpiles RUNNING LOW after 2024-2025 Red Sea campaign + Iran supply disrupted by war. Possible US/Saudi deal. Stimson: 'must decide — join Iran's war or abandon Iran.' Houthi leader al-Houthi: 'hands on trigger.' BUT: Israeli 2025 strikes decapitated Sanaa leadership, southern secessionist threat diverts focus. FDD: 'rhetorical solidarity' only. This is CRITICAL — if Houthis stay out, Red Sea remains open = alternative route preserved. If they enter: both Hormuz AND Bab el-Mandeb closed simultaneously = unprecedented global shipping crisis.

ISFAHAN UNDERGROUND NUCLEAR — NEW FACILITY: IAEA confirms Iran has NEW uranium enrichment site at underground Isfahan complex. Grossi: 'many questions, underground, haven't visited.' E3 to IAEA Board (Mar 2026): 'increasing risk of diversion, particularly around Isfahan Fuel Enrichment Plant.' Would be Iran's FOURTH enrichment plant. IAEA observing 'regular vehicular activity around entrance to tunnel complex.' 440kg at 60% = ~10 weapons. Grossi: 'war cannot entirely eliminate nuclear program — vast, scattered, decades of effort.' This UPGRADES nuclear risk: Iran may be enriching in facility coalition has never struck.

DESERTIONS GRANULAR DATA: Iran International + Militarnyi detail: Police special units — salary payments DISRUPTED, personnel declined pro-government rallies. One base: ~350 police left posts. Some units: absence/desertion approaching 90%. Retirees + army personnel NOT PAID for 2nd consecutive month. IRGC recalling retirees to active duty. Prisoners offered amnesty for cooperation. Financial strain + supply crisis + two-front war = institutional breakdown accelerating.

LEBANON ESCALATING: Health Ministry Mar 2-16: 886 killed (67 women, 111 children, 38 health workers), 2,141 wounded. Now ~1,000+ with D17-D22 strikes. WHO: 28 healthcare attacks, 30 killed. 40 health workers killed total. Amnesty: halt attacks on healthcare. Israel planning MASSIVE Litani ground invasion (Axios: 'seize entire area south of Litani River'). Lebanese govt banned Hezbollah military activities (Mar 2), ordered IRGC deportation (Mar 5). Domestic backlash against Hezbollah (WashPost: 'anger even among Shiite supporters'). 85-90K crossed to Syria.

OIL CONSOLIDATING D22: Brent ~$115 (OilPriceAPI $116.01, steady from D21). Nowruz + weekend = reduced trading. Fortune Mar 19: $113.71. March avg $93.05. IEA: 'largest supply disruption in history — 20 Mb/d offline.' Gulf output declined 21M→14M bbl/day (Rystad). EIA: >$95 next 2 months, then <$80 Q3 if conflict ends. Citi: $120+ if continues.

HORMUZ STATUS: Still ~21-23 tankers since D1. Iran allowing 'permission-based transits to friendly countries' — growing number via Iranian territorial waters. Haass editorial (Mar 18): 'Open for All or Closed to All' — proposed no Iranian tanker permitted until Iran backs off. Lloyd's: 8-10 destroyers needed per convoy = 10% of pre-war traffic at best. France + UK + Germany + Italy working on escort framework (Operation Aspides expansion). US Navy escort 'could take month or more.'

HENGAW 5TH REPORT: 5,300 killed through D18 (511 civilians = 9.6%, 4,789 military). Extrapolating to D22: ~5,800+. HRANA: 3,114 (43% civilian ratio). Health Ministry: 1,444 official + 18,551 injured. 70,000+ civilian structures damaged Mar 3-17. 251 medical centers, 498 schools, 17 Red Crescent facilities damaged. 3.2M displaced.

INTERNET BLACKOUT ~504+ HOURS (22nd day). NetBlocks: connectivity at 4% of normal. 'Longest recorded shutdown in Iran's history.' Regime allowing only select users access. 350 Starlink terminals confiscated. 21 arrested sending videos abroad.

PATRIOT SUPPLY CRISIS (Zelenskyy): Zelensky BBC interview: 'For Putin, a long war in Iran is a plus.' 800+ Patriot missiles fired D1. 60-65/month production. Putin counting on drain. Western air defense stockpiles structurally unsustainable.
WARTIME PROSECUTION: Protesters detained to June 2027 under wartime conditions. Unprecedented repression tool.

DIPLOMACY: Araghchi: 'never asked for ceasefire.' Third round indirect talks (Oman-mediated, Geneva) — no breakthrough. Araghchi offered to 'dilute enriched material' — but continued attacks undermine. Trump: 'eager to negotiate, not ready.' Lebanese govt ready for direct ceasefire talks with Israel 'immediately and without preconditions.' Macron + several states: 'defensive escort mission' framework (Operation Aspides). Haass: 'Open for All or Closed to All.'

dailyRows.capability: 25+60. IRGC fires 10+ HYPERSONIC missiles at Israel — capability escalation. Israel critically low on ballistic interceptors (Semafor). IDF: 7,600+ strikes in Iran, 1,100+ in Lebanon cumul.
dailyRows.cost: 15+ killed Isfahan. Shiraz residential areas hit. 10,000+ homes destroyed (Tehran governor). 56 cultural sites damaged. Pope calls ceasefire. Pahlavi outlines transition. Houthis threaten Red Sea resumption.
dailyRows.assessment: Hypersonic escalation + interceptor crisis = war's most dangerous moment since D1. Houthi threat = potential second maritime chokepoint.

dailyRows.primary: Israel new Tehran strikes (smoke over capital). Dubai airport drone fire (flights suspended→limited). Abu Dhabi missile kill. Fujairah drone fire. Saudi 37 drones intercepted (AJ). Baghdad airport rockets. Israel: 245 attack waves total.
dailyRows.capability: DRONE VOLUME SURGED — Saudi 37 drones alone is highest single-day count from kingdom. IRGC claims most weapons cache intact and hasn't used post-2025 missiles yet. If true, Iran's offensive depth is much greater than ↓90% suggests. Israel planning 3+ more weeks of strikes.
dailyRows.cost: Brent $106+. Gulf exports ↓61% (Reuters/Kpler). US wounded revised to ~200. Hormuz coalition failing — Australia, Japan, Germany all declined. G5 'gravely concerned' re Lebanon. Trump threatens NATO umbrella + Xi summit.
dailyRows.assessment: IRGC 'arsenal intact' claim is the most significant D17 development. If true, Iran has been deliberately rationing — using old stock while preserving modern missiles. This reframes the entire attrition narrative. War may have much longer tail than assumed.

dailyRows.primary: DECAPITATION: Larijani + Soleimani confirmed killed. UAE full airspace closure. Qatar 13/14 ballistic intercepted. Saudi 12+ drones. Baghdad Al-Rasheed Hotel + embassy targeted. Abu Dhabi 1 killed. Tanker struck off Fujairah. Israel ground invasion of Lebanon.
dailyRows.capability: Decapitation accelerating but Qatar 13/14 ballistic missiles = Iran still has meaningful ballistic capability. IRGC 'cache intact' claim from D16 gaining credibility. Israel ground ops in Lebanon = second full front.
dailyRows.cost: Joe Kent (NCTC director) resigned — 'Iran not a threat.' Trump: don't need NATO. Delayed China trip 1 month. France: 'never' Hormuz during war. Amnesty: 170+ schoolgirls killed in US strike.
dailyRows.assessment: Crosscurrents: Military decapitation vs economic leverage. Israel destroying command structure but Iran's remaining capability + Hormuz control = strategic stalemate. First tanker transit could signal beginning of negotiated passage framework.

dailyRows.primary: ENERGY + NUCLEAR WAR: Israel struck South Pars (AM). Iran hit Ras Laffan LNG TWICE (PM). Bushehr NPP 'hostile projectile' (IAEA: no reactor damage, Rosatom suspended construction). 5th senior kill (Basij head + Khatib). Kuwait 4 ballistic + 20 drones. Multi-warhead at Israel (2 killed). Saudi 4 ballistic at Riyadh DURING FM meeting + drone at gas facility.
dailyRows.capability: ENERGY WAR FULLY MUTUAL + NUCLEAR DIMENSION. Ras Laffan x2, South Pars, Bushehr NPP all hit in single day. Iran named specific Gulf targets (Samref, Jubail, Mesaieed, Al Hosn). Multi-warhead Khorramshahr-4 + Qadr. Israel striking northern Iran. D19 missile count REVISED UP from 35→40 on 2nd Ras Laffan confirmation.
dailyRows.cost: Brent $107.38 close (CNBC), spiked $111+ after-hours. Gas $3.84 (+29%). Fed held rates. Pentagon $200B+. Qatar expelled attaches. Saudi: 'right to military action.' 12 FM joint statement. E3 backing military defense. Hengaw: 5,300 dead. Lebanon 880+. Kataib Hezbollah conditional pause. IMO emergency meeting.
dailyRows.assessment: INFLECTION: Energy + nuclear. Bushehr projectile = nuclear-adjacent escalation. Ras Laffan x2 = Qatar's LNG industry threatened. E3 military backing + 12 FM statement + Saudi warning = diplomatic momentum toward intervention OR escalation. Kataib Hezbollah pause offer = first crack in Iran proxy unity. IMO corridor = international shipping institutions mobilizing. Every constraint dissolving simultaneously.

dailyRows.primary: SAMREF HIT + ENERGY WAR CONTINUES: Kuwait TWO refineries ablaze (Mina al-Ahmadi + Mina Abdullah). QatarEnergy: 'several' LNG facilities. SAMREF refinery (Yanbu, Red Sea) hit by drone — IRGC named target EXECUTED. 6th salvo at Israel. Kuwait 4 ballistic + 25+ drones. Saudi Al-Kharj 2 killed. Gabbard contradicts Trump. IAEA: 440kg = ~10 weapons. Bandar Anzali heavy bombardment.
dailyRows.capability: D20 REVISED: 45 missiles + 80 drones. SAMREF = FIRST Red Sea coast target. IRGC executing named target list: Kuwait ✓ Qatar ✓ SAMREF ✓. Remaining: Jubail, Mesaieed, Al Hosn. 6 salvos at Israel since midnight = maintaining pressure. CENTCOM: 7,800+ targets. Bandar Anzali warships destroyed.
dailyRows.cost: Brent surged $119.11 intraday (CNBC), settled ~$114 close. Citi: $120+. SAMREF 'impact minimal' per Aramco but symbolic significance enormous — Red Sea coast reached. Gabbard contradicts Trump. Kent FBI probe. Saudi FM 'trust shattered.' Pezeshkian resignation crisis. Swedish execution. Pentagon $200B+.
dailyRows.assessment: SAMREF is the inflection within the inflection. IRGC's named targets are being systematically executed. Red Sea coast = drones can reach Saudi Arabia's western export infrastructure, not just Gulf-facing facilities. This means pipeline bypass routes (East-West Pipeline to Yanbu) are no longer safe. The energy war has NO geographic limit within Saudi Arabia. Combined with Kuwait refineries + Qatar LNG = comprehensive assault on Gulf energy architecture.

dailyRows.primary: NOWRUZ — DAY 21 + MOJTABA ABSENT + IRGC FRACTURES: Hard deadline passed — no video Nowruz message from Supreme Leader. Chaharshanbe Suri 'Night of Defiance' — 'Death to Mojtaba' + 'Long live the Shah' chants (Saadat Abad confirmed). 111 arrests in 26/31 provinces. Oman ports (Duqm, Salalah) hit — Iran DENIED responsibility. IRGC desertions DOCUMENTED (multi-source). Pezeshkian-IRGC rift over economy. Israel using social media to geolocate Basij — ~300 commanders killed. 200+ Ukrainian experts in Gulf.
dailyRows.capability: D21: 40+72 (Oman theater). IRGC FRACTURE NOW DOCUMENTED: Wikipedia article on desertions created. Multi-source confirmed (Isfahan, Tabriz). 4 diplomats asylum. IRGC executing deserters. Supply crisis: 20 bullets/2 soldiers. Army-IRGC rift: IRGC denying Artesh aid. Pezeshkian apologized for Oman but IRGC continued. US dropped 5,000-lb bunker busters on Hormuz anti-ship sites. Target execution: 4/7. ~300 Basij commanders killed via social media geolocating.
dailyRows.cost: Brent SURGING ~$116 (up from $110 overnight). Up ~80% since war began. Only 21 tankers since D1. US Navy escort 'not ready.' Chaharshanbe Suri = IRGC TWO FRONTS. 111 arrests = massive security diversion. WARTIME PROSECUTION: detained until June 2027. 350 Starlink terminals confiscated. Carnegie: 1979 potential. Hengaw: 5,300 killed. HRANA: 3,114. 3.2M displaced. Internet ~480+ hrs. PATRIOT CRISIS: 800+ fired D1, 60-65/month production (Zelenskyy). Trump: 'eager to negotiate... not ready yet.' Kataib Hezbollah 5-day pause = first proxy crack. Queen Farah: 'great awakening.'
dailyRows.assessment: NOWRUZ INFLECTION — THREE SIMULTANEOUS FRACTURES: (1) LEADERSHIP: Mojtaba absent on Nowruz = succession crisis irrefutable. Netanyahu greeting Iranian people. Israel killed Larijani = closing diplomatic avenues. (2) MILITARY: Desertions NOW DOCUMENTED (Wikipedia, multi-source). 4 diplomats asylum. IRGC executing deserters. Supply crisis: 20 bullets/2 soldiers. Army-IRGC rift. US 5,000-lb bunker busters on Hormuz missile sites. Internet ~480+ hrs (NetBlocks: longest in history). (3) POLITICAL: Pezeshkian APOLOGIZED for Oman but IRGC continued = rift PUBLIC. EU Kallas: 'exit not escalation.' Combined with Chaharshanbe Suri's nationwide defiance, oil at $116, and 21 tankers in 21 days: the regime faces its most comprehensive crisis since 1979. The question is no longer IF IRGC fractures but HOW FAST.

dailyRows.primary: NOWRUZ DAY 2 — HOUTHIS STAYING OUT + ISFAHAN UNDERGROUND + DESERTIONS ACCELERATING: Mojtaba STILL absent (Day 2 no video/audio). Houthis NOT entering war despite 22 days (AP: stockpiles low, possible US/Saudi deal). IAEA confirms NEW Isfahan underground enrichment plant — 'never visited.' Desertions accelerating: police 350 at one base, 90% absence in some units, salary disruptions, retirees unpaid 2 months. IRGC recalling retirees + offering prisoner amnesty. Lebanon approaching massive Litani invasion (Axios). Continued Gulf harassment at reduced tempo.
dailyRows.capability: D22: 35+65. IRGC launch tempo declining — desertions + supply crisis (10 rounds/soldier in some units) + Basij ~300 commanders killed = institutional degradation. Police 90% absence at some units. Financial strain: salaries unpaid. IRGC recalling retirees = desperation indicator. D16 claim 'cache intact' increasingly contradicted by operational evidence of declining capacity. BUT: autonomous units may still strike remaining targets (Jubail, Mesaieed, Al Hosn).
dailyRows.cost: Brent ~$115 consolidating. IEA: '20 Mb/d offline, largest in history.' Gulf output 21M→14M (Rystad). 70K+ civilian structures damaged in Iran. 251 medical centers, 498 schools damaged. 3.2M displaced. Internet ~504+ hrs. HOUTHI RESTRAINT = CRITICAL strategic variable: if Red Sea stays open, alternative shipping survives. If Houthis join: BOTH chokepoints closed = unprecedented global crisis. Third round Geneva talks: no breakthrough. Araghchi offered to dilute enrichment but attacks continue. Lebanese govt ready for direct ceasefire talks.
dailyRows.assessment: D22 STRATEGIC PICTURE — TWO CRITICAL VARIABLES EMERGING: (1) HOUTHI DECISION: 22 days in, Houthis have NOT attacked. AP: stockpiles low after 2024-2025 campaign. Possible US/Saudi deal. If they stay out: Red Sea open = alternative routes survive, conflict partially contained. If they enter: Hormuz + Bab el-Mandeb closed = 'for first time in modern history, both major maritime corridors simultaneously blocked.' (2) ISFAHAN UNDERGROUND: New enrichment facility IAEA has never visited. Could be operational. 440kg at 60% = ~10 weapons. War cannot eliminate scattered program (Grossi). These two variables define the war's trajectory: Houthi entry = total escalation, Isfahan breakout = nuclear crisis. Meanwhile IRGC fractures continue: desertions, salary crisis, 90% police absence — but autonomous units remain dangerous.

timeline.title: Week 3 (Mar 16–22)
timeline.body: IRGC FRACTURING + HOUTHIS OUT + ISFAHAN UNDERGROUND: Nowruz Day 2 — Mojtaba STILL absent. MAJOR: Houthis NOT entering war (22 days, AP: stockpiles low, possible deal). Isfahan underground nuclear = IAEA 'never visited.' Desertions ACCELERATING: police 350 at one base, 90% absence, salary crisis. Chaharshanbe Suri defiance. 111 arrests in 26/31 provinces. Lebanon ~1,000+, Litani invasion planned. Brent $107→$119→$116→$115. IEA: 20 Mb/d offline. Hengaw: 5,300 killed (extrapolated ~5,800). 70K+ structures damaged. Geneva talks fail. Haass: 'Open for All or Closed to All.' Target scorecard 4/7.

timeline.title: Week 4–5 (Mar 23–Apr 5)
timeline.body: IRGC remaining targets = critical: Jubail (world's largest industrial city), Mesaieed (Qatar petrochemicals), Al Hosn (UAE gas). If struck: Brent $130+, Saudi military entry triggered, total Gulf shutdown. POST-NOWRUZ PROTESTS = key variable. If Chaharshanbe Suri defiance escalates: IRGC faces two-front crisis. Mojtaba's continued absence = succession unresolved. Ukrainian anti-drone deployment: does it shift Gulf defense balance? Selective Hormuz passage: does it evolve into formal framework or collapse? Congress $200B+ supplemental fight.

timeline.title: Month 2 (Apr–May)
timeline.body: Three crises converge: (1) Energy — IRGC target execution vs improved Gulf defenses (Ukrainian experts) + Macron moratorium; (2) Nuclear — Isfahan underground + IAEA locked out = breakout window; (3) Internal — post-Nowruz protest wave + succession crisis + IRGC two-front strain. US missile supply becomes binding constraint. Hormuz permission-based framework either formalizes (China/India corridor) or collapses.

timeline.title: Month 3+ (May onward)
timeline.body: If IRGC completes target list: permanent regional restructuring. Saudi entry = wider war. If post-Nowruz internal pressure sustains: IRGC faces existential two-front challenge. Nuclear verification crisis defines decade. Oman neutrality violated = regional alignment shifts. No clean outcome available.

timeline.title: Endgame variables
timeline.body: IRGC remaining targets (Jubail, Mesaieed, Al Hosn). Post-Nowruz protest trajectory. Saudi military entry decision. Mojtaba's condition / succession. Ukrainian anti-drone impact. Selective Hormuz evolution. Macron energy moratorium. Trump South Pars trigger. US missile resupply. Isfahan nuclear. Congress $200B+. Lebanon Litani. Oman neutrality.

timeline_fa.body: انرژی + هسته‌ای + داخلی. نوروز بعد + بحران جانشینی + سپاه دو جبهه. موشک آمریکا محدود. هرمز: چین/هند کریدور.

timeline_fa.body: سپاه لیست کامل = بازسازی دائمی. نوروز بعد = فشار. عمان بی‌طرفی = تحول. هسته‌ای دهه.

timeline_fa.body: جبیل، مسیعید، الحصن. نوروز بعد اعتراض. عربستان. مجتبی. اوکراین دفاع. هرمز. ماکرون. ترامپ پارس جنوبی. موشک. اصفهان. کنگره. لبنان. عمان.

scenarioProbabilities.name: Escalation spiral / total war
scenarioProbabilities.body: DECREASED 38%→36%. IRGC fractures accelerating — desertions at 90% in some police units, salary crisis, retirees recalled = desperation. Institutional capacity to execute large-scale ops degrading. HOUTHI RESTRAINT = MAJOR factor: if Houthis stay out, escalation potential REDUCED (Red Sea open = pressure relief valve). BUT: autonomous IRGC units may still strike remaining targets (Jubail, Mesaieed, Al Hosn). Isfahan underground = nuclear wild card. If nuclear breakout detected: total escalation. Probability decreased because IRGC operational capacity is visibly degrading AND Houthi non-entry removes one escalation pathway.

scenarioProbabilities.name: Protracted attrition + internal crisis
scenarioProbabilities.body: INCREASED 24%→26%. STRONGEST GAINING SCENARIO. Evidence mounting: (1) IRGC fractures prevent decisive Iranian victory. (2) Patriot crisis prevents decisive coalition victory. (3) Houthi restraint = conflict partially contained but unresolved. (4) US Navy escort 'could take month or more.' (5) Isfahan underground = verification crisis persists. (6) Geneva talks fail. (7) Lebanese govt seeking ceasefire = front may freeze. (8) IRGC recalling retirees + offering prisoner amnesty = regime digging in for long haul. (9) IEA: 20 Mb/d offline = economic pressure mounting on BOTH sides. Mutual degradation with no exit mechanism = definition of protracted attrition.

scenarioProbabilities.name: International intervention / ceasefire
scenarioProbabilities.body: INCREASED 15%→16%. Multiple frameworks advancing: Haass 'Open for All or Closed to All.' France + UK + Germany + Italy: Operation Aspides escort expansion. Lebanese govt: direct ceasefire talks 'immediately.' Geneva round 3 (Oman-mediated). Araghchi offered to dilute enrichment. IEA: 'largest supply disruption in history' = global urgency at maximum. Grossi: 'lasting solution on diplomatic table.' BUT: attacks continue despite offers. Most likely form: partial maritime corridor + Lebanon ceasefire, NOT comprehensive deal.

scenarioProbabilities.name: Declared victory + exit
scenarioProbabilities.body: UNCHANGED at 10%. IRGC fractures (90% police absence, desertions) strengthen exit narrative. Trump: 'eager to negotiate.' $200B+ cost + 13 US KIA + $115 oil. Kent FBI probe. BUT: Hormuz still closed, IRGC still launching. Houthi restraint could enable partial 'victory' claim if spun correctly.

scenarioProbabilities.name: Regime fracture / IRGC collapse
scenarioProbabilities.body: ADJUSTED 11%→10% — slight decrease. NEW DATA complicates picture: WashPost reports Western security officials 'did not see defections among high-ranking.' Desertions REAL at unit level (police 350 at one base, 90% absence) but may not reach institutional collapse threshold. IRGC adapting: recalling retirees, prisoner amnesty, executing deserters. Regime survival instincts strong. Carnegie 1979 comparison still valid for SCALE of unrest but 1979 required IRGC refusal to fire — and IRGC is still firing. Slight downward adjustment reflects Western intelligence assessment, but remains 'emerging possibility' — watch post-Nowruz protest trajectory.

scenarioProbabilities.name: Negotiated deal
scenarioProbabilities.body: UNCHANGED. Geneva round 3: no breakthrough. Araghchi offered dilution but attacks continued. Selective Hormuz passage = de facto framework without formal deal. Lebanon ceasefire talks = possible partial deal on one front. But comprehensive Iran deal: still near-zero probability.

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: مارپیچ تشدید / جنگ تمام‌عیار
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: کاهش ۳۸→۳۶. سپاه تحلیل + حوثی بیرون = تشدید کاهش. اما واحدهای مستقل + اصفهان هسته‌ای = خطر.

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: فرسایشی + بحران داخلی
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: افزایش ۲۴→۲۶. قوی‌ترین سناریو. فرسایش دوطرفه. ژنو شکست. IEA: ۲۰ میلیون آفلاین. رژیم حفر.

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: مداخله بین‌المللی
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: افزایش ۱۵→۱۶. هاس + ماکرون + اسکورت. لبنان مذاکره مستقیم. گروسی: 'دیپلماسی.' اما حمله ادامه.

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: اعلام پیروزی + خروج
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: ثابت. سپاه شکاف = 'مأموریت تمام.' ترامپ: 'مشتاق.' ۲۰۰+ میلیارد. اما هرمز بسته.

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: شکست رژیم / فروپاشی سپاه
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: تنظیم ۱۱→۱۰. واشنگتن‌پست: 'فرار بالارتبه ندید.' اما واحد = ۹۰٪ غیبت. رژیم سازگار: بازنشسته + زندانی. نوروز بعد = متغیر.

scenarioProbabilities_fa.name: مذاکره
scenarioProbabilities_fa.body: ژنو ۳: شکست. عراقچی رقیق‌سازی اما حمله. لبنان = ممکن جزئی.

theoryEvaluation.title: HOUTHI RESTRAINT — the dog that didn't bark
theoryEvaluation.body: 22 days into the war, Yemen's Houthis have NOT attacked — despite rhetoric and expectations. This is arguably the most significant strategic development since Week 1. Analysis: (1) AP reports Houthi stockpiles RUNNING LOW after 2024-2025 Red Sea campaign; Iran supply disrupted by war. (2) Possible US/Saudi deal — Houthis may have been given assurances in exchange for restraint. (3) Israeli 2025 strikes decapitated Sanaa military leadership — deterrence effect. (4) Southern secessionist threat diverts Houthi focus internally. (5) Stimson Center: 'must decide — join Iran's war or abandon Iran.' STRATEGIC IMPLICATIONS: If Houthis stay out, Red Sea shipping remains viable as alternative route. The war's economic damage, while severe, stays partially contained to Hormuz. If Houthis enter: Hormuz + Bab el-Mandeb closed = 'first time in modern history, both major maritime corridors simultaneously blocked' = $150+ oil, global recession trigger. The Times: Houthis awaiting Iranian signal if US weakens Hormuz control. THIS IS THE SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT VARIABLE for whether war remains regional or becomes global crisis.

theoryEvaluation.title: Isfahan underground — nuclear verification crisis deepens
theoryEvaluation.body: IAEA confirms Iran has NEW uranium enrichment site at underground Isfahan complex. This is Iran's FOURTH known enrichment plant, and IAEA has 'never visited' it. E3 statement to IAEA Board (Mar 2026): 'increasing risk of diversion, particularly around Isfahan Fuel Enrichment Plant.' Regular vehicular activity observed at entrance. Grossi: war 'cannot entirely eliminate nuclear program — vast, scattered, decades of effort.' Araghchi offered to dilute enrichment at Geneva, but attacks continued, undermining credibility. 440kg at 60% = ~10 weapons-worth. Combined with IRGC fractures: WHO controls nuclear assets in a fragmenting command? If breakout detected: could trigger Israeli nuclear preemption scenario. Nuclear dimension is ESCALATING even as conventional capacity degrades.

theoryEvaluation.title: IRGC institutional degradation — detailed picture
theoryEvaluation.body: New granular data reveals IRGC degradation is deeper than initially assessed: (1) Police special units: salary payments DISRUPTED, some units 90% absence/desertion. ~350 police left posts at single base. (2) Military: 10 rounds per soldier, no water/food in some units. Retirees not paid 2 consecutive months. (3) IRGC response: recalling retirees to active duty + offering prisoners amnesty for cooperation = desperation measures. (4) Basij: ~300 commanders killed via social media geolocating. (5) Diplomats: 4 seeking Western asylum. (6) Army-IRGC rift: IRGC denying Artesh supplies. HOWEVER: WashPost cites Western officials who 'did not see defections among high-ranking military.' The picture = unit-level collapse alongside institutional survival. IRGC leadership holds, but enforcement capacity at ground level is degrading. This matters for: ability to suppress post-Nowruz protests, sustain Hormuz operations, execute remaining target list. The question: can institutional command survive unit-level hemorrhaging?

theoryEvaluation_fa.title: حوثی بیرون — سگی که پارس نکرد
theoryEvaluation_fa.body: ۲۲ روز، حوثی حمله نکرد. AP: ذخایر کم. احتمال توافق آمریکا/عربستان. اسرائیل ۲۰۲۵ رهبری صنعا. اگر بیرون: دریای سرخ باز. اگر وارد: هرمز + باب‌المندب = بحران بی‌سابقه جهانی. مهم‌ترین متغیر.

theoryEvaluation_fa.title: اصفهان زیرزمینی — بحران هسته‌ای عمیق‌تر
theoryEvaluation_fa.body: IAEA: تأسیسات جدید اصفهان. 'هرگز بازدید.' چهارم. ۴۴۰ کیلو = ۱۰ سلاح. گروسی: 'جنگ حذف نمی‌کند.' عراقچی: رقیق‌سازی اما حمله ادامه. سپاه شکاف = چه کسی کنترل هسته‌ای؟

theoryEvaluation_fa.title: سپاه تحلیل نهادی — تصویر دقیق
theoryEvaluation_fa.body: پلیس: ۹۰٪ غیبت، ۳۵۰ ترک. حقوق ۲ ماه. سپاه: بازنشسته فراخوان + زندانی عفو. بسیج ۳۰۰. ۱۰ گلوله/سرباز. اما واشنگتن‌پست: بالارتبه فرار ندید. = فروپاشی واحد + بقای نهادی.

endstate.title: Most likely (36%)
endstate.body: Escalation spiral — DECREASED 38%→36%. IRGC operational capacity visibly degrading (90% police absence, salary crisis, retirees recalled). HOUTHI RESTRAINT removes one escalation pathway (Red Sea stays open). BUT: autonomous IRGC units + Isfahan underground nuclear = wild cards. If remaining targets hit OR nuclear breakout detected: instant escalation. Decreasing because capacity is degrading AND Houthi non-entry = partial containment.

endstate.title: Second (26%)
endstate.body: Protracted attrition + internal crisis — INCREASED 24%→26%. STRONGEST GAINING SCENARIO. Geneva talks fail. IEA: 20 Mb/d offline. IRGC recalling retirees = regime digging in. Patriot crisis = coalition also structurally limited. Lebanon ceasefire talks = possible partial freeze. Houthi restraint = war doesn't become global but doesn't resolve. Neither side achieves decisive outcome. Mutual degradation with no exit mechanism.

endstate.title: Emerging (16%)
endstate.body: International intervention — INCREASED 15%→16%. Multiple frameworks advancing: Haass 'Open for All or Closed to All,' Aspides escort expansion, Lebanese direct ceasefire, Grossi diplomatic call. IEA 'largest disruption in history' = maximum urgency. Most likely form: partial maritime corridor + Lebanon ceasefire, NOT comprehensive deal.

endstate.title: Emerging (10%)
endstate.body: Regime fracture — ADJUSTED 11%→10%. WashPost: Western officials 'did not see high-ranking defections.' Unit-level collapse real (90% police absence) but institutional command holding. IRGC adapting with retirees + prisoner amnesty. 1979 comparison: scale of unrest similar but IRGC still firing (1979 required military refusal). Post-Nowruz protests = THE variable. If sustained: upward revision.

endstate_fa.title: محتمل‌ترین (۳۶٪)
endstate_fa.body: تشدید — کاهش ۳۸→۳۶. سپاه تحلیل + حوثی بیرون. اما واحدهای مستقل + اصفهان هسته‌ای.

endstate_fa.title: دوم (۲۶٪)
endstate_fa.body: فرسایشی — افزایش ۲۴→۲۶. قوی‌ترین. ژنو شکست. IEA: ۲۰ Mb/d. رژیم حفر. فرسایش دوطرفه.

endstate_fa.title: نوظهور (۱۶٪)
endstate_fa.body: مداخله — افزایش ۱۵→۱۶. هاس + اسکورت + لبنان مذاکره. IEA: فوریت حداکثر.

endstate_fa.title: نوظهور (۱۰٪)
endstate_fa.body: شکست سپاه — تنظیم ۱۱→۱۰. واشنگتن‌پست: بالارتبه نه. واحد = ۹۰٪. نوروز بعد = متغیر.
